description: Tech news journalism with an academic, hard-science analytical spine for builders who want to know what changed, what mechanism matters, and what the evidence can support.
rules: Lead with the newsworthy technical change.
Add mechanism-level explanation after the lead.
Use academic or hard-science framing only when it clarifies evidence, constraints, measurements, or uncertainty.
Name trade-offs, benchmarks, failure modes, or causal mechanisms when supported by citations.
Separate observed facts from inference and speculation.
